title: Minimum Wages and Conditions of Employment (Truck and Bus Drivers) (Amendment) Order, 2021

sections.heading: This Order may be cited as the Minimum Wages and Title
sections.text:
Conditions of Employment (Truck and Bus Drivers) (Amendment)
Order, 2021, and shall be read as one with the Minimum Wages SI. No. 106
and Conditions of Employment (Truck and Bus Drivers) Order, of 2020
2020, in this Order referred to as the principal Order.

sections.heading: The principal Order is amended by the revocation of Revocation
sections.text:
paragraph 11 and the substitution therefor of the following: and
replacement

sections.heading: (1) An employer shall pay a driver who spends a night of paragraph
sections.text:
11
away from home to attend to any business of the employer, a
subsistence allowance of not less than three hundred kwacha
per night where—
(a) the employer does not provide, or pay for
accommodation for the driver while on duty; or
Subsistence allowance for local trip
(b) in the case of a truck driver, the truck does not
have a sleeping cabin.
(2) Despite sub-paragraph (1), an employer shall, where
the truck has a sleeping cabin, pay a truck driver who spends
a night away from home to attend to any business of the
employer a subsistence allowance of one hundred kwacha
per night.
Copies of this Statutory Instrument can be obtained from the Government Printer,
P.O. Box 30136, 10101 Lusaka. Price K4.00 each.
742 Statutory Instruments 31st December, 2021
Revocation 3. The principal Order is amended by the revocation of
and paragraph 14 and the substitution therefor of the following:
replacement
of paragraph
14
Risk 14. An employer shall pay a truck driver of dangerous
allowance goods or abnormal load a risk allowance of fifteen ngwee
per kilometre for a local trip and eight ngwee per kilometre
for an international trip, respectively.
Revocation 4. The principal Order is amended by the revocation of
and paragraph 15 and the substitution therefor of the following:
replacement
of paragraph
15
Cross 15. (1) An employer shall pay a driver who undertakes
border an international trip, a cross border subsistence allowance of
subsistence
allowance not less than twenty-five United States dollars per night where
the truck has no sleeping cabin, or the employer has not
provided or paid for accommodation.
(2) Despite sub-paragraph (1), an employer shall, where
a truck has a sleeping cabin, pay a truck driver who
undertakes an international trip a subsistence allowance of
fifteen United States dollars per night.
B. M. TAMBATAMBA,
LUSAKA Minister of Labour and
22nd December, 2021 Social Security
[DL/64/9/9]
